title: La Divina Commedia di Dante: Complete
summaries: "La Divina Commedia di Dante: Complete" by Dante Alighieri is a narrative poem written between 1308 and 1321. It chronicles Dante's journey through the three realms of the afterlife: Hell, Purgatory, and Heaven. Guided by the poet Virgil and his beloved Beatrice, Dante encounters souls receiving divine justice based on their earthly actions. The work allegorically represents the soul's journey toward God, drawing on medieval Catholic theology and philosophy to explore sin, redemption, and salvation.
